steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage introduces Camacho and McLachlan with 'But,' signaling a challenge to the invasive/native framework. Their point is that climate change shifts species ranges and creates new habitats, which destabilizes fixed classifications. The correct choice must follow logically from that challenge.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the framework is partially useful: Hedges rather than confronts the problem. Camacho and McLachlan's argument targets the framework's validity, not its degree of usefulness. ✗
  • Choice B — a false history of the framework: Invents claims not supported by the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — fixed classifications must be reevaluated: Follows directly from the premise that climate change reshapes where species live, making current invasive/native labels unreliable. ✓
  • Choice D — focuses on coordination among researchers: Addresses an organizational issue rather than the core problem of flawed classification criteria. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
